ads.author: Dynel Matvia
ads.title: 1988 "Taxi" fully restored pinball machine, works great
ads.description: My father was an electrician in the steel mill for over 42 years. He specialized in curvuit6 boards, cpu boards and soundboards. He has done every board that you could possibly think of 4 electrical work. He had been fully restoring and completely renovating to its original form of every pin machine that he worked on , which was so many that I could lost count years ago.. He was very meticulous and particular about perfection on each of his machines than he did for people. Pinball machines were his pride and joy as his side job from the mill on top of that , he also could build classic cars from the ground up. He was definitely a workaholic, but he loved it.Even until 2 days he went into the hospital.He was still working on pinball machines, All the way up until then. Taxi is definitely unique and definitely fun. I have it set on free play. Comes with 4 free new legs that our universal legs. This was one of his favorite machines because it is the first machine he worked on and fully restored when he came to florida from indiana because he had his own side business called rick's pinball in indiana. My dad also did a lot of work for a very well-known pinball arcade owner in the Fort Myers area. And they were very close. This machine is from 1988, made by Williams. And has marilyn, Lola, and Santa and a vampire, so it's kind of a comedy, but it's a very fun game, probably one of the funnest games I've ever played. My dad changed all the lights that originally came with the machine to LED lights.He does that with all his machines before he died in two thousand twenty four from cancer. I real. Ly hate to have to get rid of this machine but I've seen a lot of pinball machines in my lifetime and I think it's time to let it go, but it was his last machine that he owned, and it meant a lot to him. So I'm asking 3500I will post the price. Range from pin side and I'm willing to negotiate a small amount, but not much. Because it is in perfect condition, he also redid the cabinet. All the boards are perfect. I mean, like pristine, because that's what he specializes in so I am located in Lehigh Acres, east kind of near Pelican, snowballs and Richmond sort of 6th street. I do have some extra boards here. Depending on what boards they are, some may just come with it and some may cost a little, but some of the boards mightbbe extras for parts or ever if you might ever need them. And I also have a small bag of pinball parts of these random things that you might need in LED lights. So if you're interested, please let me know if I don't get to backseat right away, I will be back to you as soon as I see it like I said, asking 3500 and I know it's going to be very hard to ever find Marilyn on a pinball machine without her permission, so I actually heard that they got in trouble for doing that so So that's definitely a collector.

ads.author: Eric Sturdivant
ads.title: Alive Pinball Machine
ads.description: Alive is a solid-state pinball machine produced by Brunswick Manufacturing Co. in 1978. It was designed specifically as an inexpensive "home use only" model rather than a commercial arcade machine, which is reflected in its smaller cabinet size and lack of a coin slot.The "Elvis" ConnectionThe machine is most famous for its unofficial association with Elvis Presley.Design Background: It was originally intended to be an Elvis-themed machine. However, after Elvis died in 1977, Brunswick reportedly could not secure the rights to his name or image.The "Look-alike": To circumvent legal issues, the Artist's Depiction on the backglass features an Elvis-like figure with a hairstyle more reminiscent of Tom Jones, another popular 1970s icon.Gameplay and FeaturesElectronic Scoring: It features digital displays for up to four players and includes electronic scoring sounds and seven rock-style songs.Playfield Layout: The relatively simple field includes two flippers, one "thumper-bumper" (pop bumper), a manual plunger, and a spinner.Targets: Players can aim for specific rollover switches and targets to earn double or triple bonus points.Build Quality: Because it was a home model, it was built with lighter materials like plywood and "sawdust and glue" rather than heavy-duty arcade-grade wood. Recently Serviced

ads.author: Mike Mackey
ads.title: Atari Kangaroo Arcade Game – Beautiful Cabinet – $600
ads.description: For Sale: Offered for sale is a very clean, original Atari Kangaroo upright arcade game. The cabinet presents beautifully. The interior has been thoroughly cleaned, and the entire wire harness has been cleaned and detailed. The marquee glass and outer bezel glass are both in excellent condition with no paint flaking. The only cosmetic detraction is that the coin door could use a fresh coat of paint. The game features its original monitor, which has been fully recapped, including a new filter capacitor, producing a bright, crisp picture with excellent color. The regulator/audio board has been completely rebuilt, and the power brick/power supply section has also been rebuilt, including a new Big Blue filter capacitor and bridge rectifier, making the monitor and power systems ready for years of reliable service. After the rebuilds, the game played perfectly with excellent picture quality and sound. It currently has a startup issue where it usually boots to the familiar "jail bars" screen instead of the game. On the occasions that it boots correctly, it plays flawlessly and continues to run without issue. Based on the troubleshooting completed so far, the intermittent Jail bar issue appears to be isolated to the game PCB stack (CPU/video boards) rather than the rebuilt monitor or power section. At this point, I'm simply ready to move on to other projects. Video showing the game running correctly, followed by the startup jail bar issue: https://youtu.be/s2AsnePrE2A?is=7U1lotOc85PwkjrG Price: $600 FIRM Pickup: Nicholasville, Kentucky (warehouse location across from Sam's Club) This is an excellent opportunity to own one of Atari's classic early-1980s arcade games. Over two weeks of restoration work has already been invested. The cabinet has been cleaned inside and out, the wiring harness has been detailed, the monitor has been fully recapped (including the filter capacitor), and both the regulator/audio board and power brick have been rebuilt. The remaining issue appears to be confined to the game electronics, making this an excellent project for an arcade hobbyist or technician looking to finish a very nice example. I would prefer not to part it out, although I could easily make more money doing so. I'd much rather see this machine restored and enjoyed. At $600, It's an excellent value.

ads.author: Rebecca Gabel
ads.title: 1963 Corvette Cross County Bally Pinball WORKING, UNRESTORED
ads.description: 1963 Bally Cross Country Pinball Machine – Original & Working – Only 500 Produced Offered for sale is an exceptionally rare 1963 Bally Cross Country electro-mechanical pinball machine. Designed by the legendary Ted Zale, Cross Country was Bally’s second flipper game after returning to the flipper market in 1963 and is widely recognized as Ted Zale’s first original Bally design. With a documented production run of just 500 machines, it is one of the scarcest early Bally EM games and is seldom offered for sale. This machine is working and retains its original character, making it an outstanding addition to any serious Bally or early electro-mechanical collection. Features: * Manufactured by Bally in March 1963 * Original Ted Zale design * Only 500 produced * Electro-mechanical score reels * Original artwork and classic early Bally gameplay * Fully working Whether you’re a collector of early Bally machines, a Ted Zale enthusiast, or simply appreciate historically significant pinball machines, opportunities to acquire a Cross Country are few and far between. The machine presents well, plays as it should, and is ready to enjoy or display. Additional photos, gameplay video, and specific condition details are available upon request. Serious inquiries are welcome.

ads.author: Mike Mackey
ads.title: Nintendo HELIFIRE 1980 arcade cocktail game - ONLY 2017 Plays
ads.description: (Located near Lexington, KY) VERY RARE cocktail version of game only 2017 plays on coin counter. Nintendo HeliFire is rarely found in the wild—it’s the rarest of Nintendo’s early video arcade lineup. It came out after Radar Scope, both released in 1980, and prior to Donkey Kong, the game that made Nintendo famous. HeliFire had the “TUB-up” designation stamped on both the cabinet top plate and the monitor frame. This example has both, so it’s definitely a legit Nintendo build and not a conversion. And far rarer, than the already much sought after upright cabinet; because cocktail are produced in a much smaller percentage than uprights. Everything is original. Has switching power supply installed, but includes the original power supply as well. All boards have been serviced: socketed chips removed and legs cleaned, all tantalum caps replaced, nearly all electrolytic caps replaced. Monitor has been recapped. It has run for multiple hours at a time in attract mode and in play, both to burn in and to flush out any potential issues with the servicing. No problems have occurred. There are only 13 acknowledged owners of HeliFire in the VAPS (Video Arcade Preservation Society) census (combined uprights and cocktails). The VAPS numbers are a bit confusing because they only specify member activity from the past three years. Current VAPS stats list 3 uprights and 3 cocktails. Of course, there are likely more examples out in the wild, as not everyone records their serial numbers with VAPS. Walkthrough vid here: https://youtu.be/7-H8C4wyWbQ?si=xUoLG-4BUlTYWABm I will palletize for $30. I have palletized and shipped numerous arcade/pinball games with no issues, and my palletizing is far better than any shipper. Guaranteed secure payment requested (cash, bank wire transfer or equivalent). Pick-up available at our Winchester, KY warehouse. FaceTime walk-through available upon request. Any questions are welcome.
